title: Administer Tactic Types and Delivery Methods

story.so_that: manage which tactic types and delivery methods are available for requestors to select in order forms
story.i_want: add, remove, turn on or turn off tactic types and their linked delivery methods (mail, mobile app, in-story kiosk)

journey.before: Administrator needs to configure the system to control which tactic types and delivery methods are available to requestors
journey.during: Administrator navigates to the Tactic Type & Delivery Method section, reviews current settings, makes changes in draft mode, and saves when ready
journey.after: Changes take effect immediately, affecting what requestors can select in order forms while preserving historical data

capability_breakdown.core_action: Manage tactic types and their associated delivery methods through an admin interface with draft-save workflow
inputs: Parent type reference for delivery methods
outputs: List of tactic types with linked delivery methods
state_changes: Draft changes persisted to database on save

ui: Tactic Type & Delivery Method management screen
database: delivery_methods table (id, name, parent_type_id, requires_print_support, is_active, created_at, updated_at)
draft_changes table or versioning system (to track draft state)
audit_log table (to track changes and preserve history)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Administrator can view tactic types and delivery methods in list format
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given I am an Administrator
When I navigate to the Tactic Type & Delivery Method section
Then I see a list of tactic types with their linked delivery methods
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: View available tactic types and delivery methods

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Active or inactive status is visible for each tactic type and delivery method
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
When I view the tactic types list
Then each tactic type and delivery method shows its active/inactive status
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Identify which tactic types and delivery methods are currently active

acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
When I click on a tactic type or delivery method
Then I can toggle its active/inactive status
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Toggle active/inactive status for tactic types and delivery methods

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Administrator can add new tactic types and delivery methods
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
When I click add new tactic type or delivery method
Then I can enter the required information and save it
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Create new tactic types and delivery methods

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Active tactic types and methods are visible to requestors in order forms
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given a tactic type and delivery method is marked active
When a requestor views the order form
Then they can select that tactic type and delivery method
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Make tactic types and delivery methods available to requestors

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Inactive tactic types and methods are not visible to any users
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given a tactic type and delivery method is marked inactive
When a requestor or any user views the order form
Then they cannot select that tactic type and delivery method
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Hide tactic types and delivery methods from requestors

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Inactive tactic types and methods remain in historical records
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given a tactic type and delivery method is marked inactive
When viewing historical requests that used that tactic/method
Then the historical record still shows the tactic type and delivery method
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Preserve historical data when deactivating tactic types and delivery methods

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Delivery methods are unique by Name and ParentType combination
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
When creating a delivery method
Then the combination of Name and ParentType must be unique

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Tactic types and delivery methods cannot be deleted, only marked inactive
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
When managing tactic types or delivery methods
Then deletion is not permitted, only marking as inactive
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Prevent accidental data loss by disallowing deletion

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Only tactic types with at least one delivery method are displayed
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
When displaying tactic types in the admin interface
Then only show tactic types that have at least one associated delivery method
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Filter tactic types to show only those with delivery methods

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Changes are held in draft mode until explicitly saved
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
When making changes to tactic types or delivery methods
Then changes are held in draft mode until explicitly saved
And users can review all changes before committing
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Review and confirm changes before saving

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Delivery methods can specify print support requirement
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
When creating or editing a delivery method
Then I can specify whether it requires print support
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Specify print support requirement for delivery methods

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Delivery methods have active or inactive status
acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
When managing delivery methods
Then each method has an active or inactive status
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Track active/inactive status for delivery methods

edge_cases.criterion: Attempting to create delivery method with duplicate Name+ParentType
edge_cases.gherkin:
Given a delivery method with Name 'Email' and ParentType 'Direct' exists
When I attempt to create another delivery method with the same Name and ParentType
Then an error message is displayed and the creation is prevented

edge_cases.criterion: Attempting to save draft with invalid data
edge_cases.gherkin:
Given I have made changes with missing required fields
When I click Save
Then validation errors are displayed and save is prevented

Given I have made changes in draft mode
When I click Discard
Then all draft changes are lost and the interface reverts to the last saved state

edge_cases.criterion: Tactic type with no delivery methods
edge_cases.gherkin:
Given a tactic type exists with no associated delivery methods
When viewing the tactic types list
Then the tactic type is not displayed
edge_cases.jtbd: Hide tactic types without delivery methods

edge_cases.criterion: Deactivating a tactic type that is used in active orders
edge_cases.gherkin:
Given a tactic type is used in active/pending orders
When I mark it as inactive
Then the tactic type becomes unavailable for new orders but existing orders are unaffected
edge_cases.jtbd: Handle deactivation of in-use tactic types

edge_cases.gherkin:
Given two administrators are editing the same tactic type simultaneously
When both attempt to save
Then the second save is prevented or merged appropriately with conflict resolution

edge_cases.criterion: Very long tactic type or delivery method names
edge_cases.gherkin:
Given I enter a very long name (e.g., 500+ characters)
When I attempt to save
Then either the input is truncated or an error is displayed

edge_cases.criterion: Special characters in tactic type or delivery method names
edge_cases.gherkin:
Given I enter special characters (e.g., @, #, %, emoji) in a name field
When I save
Then the special characters are either accepted or rejected consistently

questions.question: What is the maximum length for tactic type and delivery method names?
How should concurrent edits by multiple administrators be handled?
Should there be a confirmation dialog when deactivating a tactic type or delivery method that is in use?
What is the scope of 'draft mode'? Does it apply to the entire form or individual changes?
Should administrators be able to see which orders/requests use a specific tactic type or delivery method?
What audit trail or logging is required for tactic type and delivery method changes?
Are there any role-based access controls needed beyond 'Administrator'?

new_code: Admin UI components for tactic management

blocked_by: Authentication/authorization system must be in place
Admin interface framework must be established
blocks: Requestor order form feature (depends on available tactic types and delivery methods)
Reporting/analytics features that reference tactic types and delivery methods

domain_knowledge: What constitutes a 'tactic type' in the business domain
What constitutes a 'delivery method' in the business domain
Business rules for tactic type and delivery method combinations
How tactic types and delivery methods are used in order workflows
technical_knowledge: Database design patterns for hierarchical data (tactic types and delivery methods)
stakeholder_questions: What are the specific tactic types and delivery methods that should be supported?
Are there any business rules about which tactic types can have which delivery methods?
What is the expected frequency of changes to tactic types and delivery methods?
Should there be notifications when tactic types or delivery methods are deactivated?

open_questions.question: What is the maximum length for tactic type and delivery method names?
How should concurrent edits by multiple administrators be handled?
Should there be a confirmation dialog when deactivating a tactic type or delivery method that is in use?
What is the scope of 'draft mode'? Does it apply to the entire form or individual changes?
Should administrators be able to see which orders/requests use a specific tactic type or delivery method?
What audit trail or logging is required for tactic type and delivery method changes?
Are there any role-based access controls needed beyond 'Administrator'?

recommendation: Proceed with implementation. This is a moderately complex feature with clear requirements. The main uncertainties are around draft mode scope and concurrent editing, which should be clarified with stakeholders before implementation begins. The story has good acceptance criteria coverage and identified edge cases.
